com.marklogic.spring.batch.item.rdbms

Class AllTablesItemReader

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.springframework.batch.item.ItemStreamSupport
      • org.springframework.batch.item.support.AbstractItemStreamItemReader<java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object>>
        • com.marklogic.spring.batch.item.rdbms.AllTablesItemReader
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    org.springframework.batch.item.ItemReader<java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object>>, org.springframework.batch.item.ItemStream, org.springframework.batch.item.ItemStreamReader<java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object>>

    public class AllTablesItemReader
extends org.springframework.batch.item.support.AbstractItemStreamItemReader<java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object>>
    Spring Batch Reader that first queries for all the table names from the given DataSource, and then reads rows from every table.

    The excludeTableNames property can be used to exclude certain table names from processing.

    The tableQueries property can be used to specify a custom SELECT query for a particular table name. By default, "SELECT * FROM (table name)" is used.

    • Constructor Detail

      • AllTablesItemReader

    • Method Detail

      • open

        public void open(org.springframework.batch.item.ExecutionContext executionContext)
          throws org.springframework.batch.item.ItemStreamException
        Use the DataSource to get a list of all the tables. Then, create a JdbcCursorItemReader for every table with a SQL query of "SELECT * FROM (table)". Put each of those into a List. Set a RowColumnRowMapper on each JdbcCursorItemReader so that every row is read as a ColumnMap.
        Specified by:
        open in interface org.springframework.batch.item.ItemStream
        Overrides:
        open in class org.springframework.batch.item.ItemStreamSupport
        Throws:
        org.springframework.batch.item.ItemStreamException

      • read

        public java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object> read()
                                                      throws java.lang.Exception
        Reads a row from the active JdbcCursorItemReader. If that returns null, move on to the next JdbcCursorItemReader. If there are no more readers, then this method is all done.
        Throws:
        java.lang.Exception

      • addTableQuery

        public void addTableQuery(java.lang.String tableName,
                          java.lang.String sql)
        Register a custom SQL query for selecting data from the given table name. By default, a query of "SELECT * FROM (table name)" is used.
        Parameters:
        tableName -
        sql -

      • getTableNames

        protected java.util.List<java.lang.String> getTableNames()
        Returns:
        a list of the table names, retrieved via the connection metadata object. The excludeTableNames property can be used to ignore certain table names.

      • buildTableReader

        protected org.springframework.batch.item.database.JdbcCursorItemReader<java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.Object>> buildTableReader(java.lang.String tableName,
                                                                                                                                          org.springframework.batch.item.ExecutionContext executionContext)
        Parameters:
        tableName -
        executionContext -
        Returns:
        a JdbcCursorItemReader for the given table name. Override this method to alert the SQL statement that's used for a particular table.

      • getSqlQueryForTable

        protected java.lang.String getSqlQueryForTable(java.lang.String tableName)
        Uses the tableQueries property to see if there's a custom SQL query for the given table name.
        Parameters:
        tableName -
        Returns:
